channel.title: NYT > World > Americas
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Hantavirus Outbreaks Are Rare, but They Aren’t Going Away and There’s No Cure
item.description: Since the family of rodent-borne infections were identified in the 1950s, they have turned up all over the world.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan
item.media:description: A cruise ship remained stranded off the coast of Cape Verde after a suspected hantavirus outbreak on the vessel killed at least three people and left several others ill.

item.title: Louise Arbour Named as Canada’s Governor General
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney’s appointment of Louise Arbour, a former Supreme Court justice, as governor general, sends a message beyond Canada.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:credit: Adrian Wyld/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada with the country’s new governor general, Louise Arbour, in Ottawa on Tuesday.

item.title: Can Javier Milei’s Deregulatory Frenzy Buoy the Global Right?
item.description: With his signature chain saw, Javier Milei became a MAGA superstar. At home, his state-slashing experiment is on the line.
item.dc:creator: Roger Cohen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: U.S. Military Strikes Boat in Caribbean, Killing 2
item.description: The aerial strike raised the death toll to at least 187 in the U.S. campaign against boats the Trump administration accuses of smuggling drugs.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: An image from a video released by U.S. Southern Command showing a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel conducted by Joint Task Force Southern Spear.

item.title: U.S. Revokes Visas of Board Members at Costa Rica’s Top Watchdog Newspaper
item.description: The move came after the paper critically covered the country’s president, who has cozied up to the United States. Analysts view the visa decision as part of a White House strategy to punish critics of its allies.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega
item.media:credit: Mark Schiefelbein/Agence France-Presse, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, left, with President Rodrigo Chaves of Costa Rica last year.

item.title: Mark Carney Pulls Canada Closer to Europe as Both Struggle With Trump
item.description: At a summit of European leaders, the Canadian prime minister was a special guest offering deals and friendship to jittery allies.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada arriving for a meeting of the European Political Community on Monday in Yerevan, Armenia.

item.title: Monster Truck Drives Into Crowd in Colombia, Killing 3
item.description: The driver of the truck appeared to lose control of the vehicle after performing a stunt at an outdoor rally in Popayán.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.media:credit: Colombia’s National Police Press, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A monster truck after an accident during a show in Popayán, Colombia, on Sunday.

item.title: What to Know About the Hantavirus Outbreak on an Atlantic Cruise Ship
item.description: Health authorities said three passengers from the MV Hondius had died after showing symptoms of the rare disease, with another critically ill and three more showing mild symptoms.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young and Claire Moses
item.media:description: The cruise ship MV Hondius stationed off the port of Praia, Cape Verde, on Sunday.

item.title: 3 Dead in Hantavirus Outbreak Aboard Cruise Ship, W.H.O. Says
item.description: One case of hantavirus infection has been confirmed in a laboratory, the organization said on Sunday. There are five additional suspected cases.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri and Livia Albeck-Ripka
item.media:description: The cruise ship MV Hondius off Cape Verde on Sunday. Officials identified the ship as the one with passengers infected by hantavirus.

item.title: Crack Down? Close Ranks? U.S. Cartel Case Puts Mexican Leaders on the Spot.
item.description: The indictment of a Mexican governor has spotlighted corruption, strained cross-border relations and handed President Claudia Sheinbaum a thorny choice.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas
item.media:credit: Carl De Souza/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Suspicions have long swirled around Rubén Rocha Moya, the governor of Sinaloa State in northwestern Mexico, home of the Sinaloa Cartel.

item.title: These Countries Embrace E.V.s to Avoid Oil Price Shocks
item.description: People in Costa Rica and other Latin American, Asian and African countries are increasingly buying electric vehicles to avoid spiking fuel prices.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing and Tony Cenicola
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

item.title: Global Demand for Rare Earth Minerals Is Intensifying Criminal Threats in the Amazon Rainforest
item.description: Global demand for critical minerals, used to build drones and electric cars, is surging, setting of a new wave of criminality in the world’s largest rainforest.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova
item.media:credit: Victor Moriyama for The New York Times
item.media:description: An illegal mine on protected land of the Yanomami people in the Brazilian Amazon.

item.title: Spirit Airlines Cancels All Flights, Stranding Passengers
item.description: The budget carrier abruptly canceled flights early on Saturday, leaving passengers to rush to make other plans. “Even if they go back into business, never again,” one traveler said.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk
item.media:description: Some passengers were in the middle of their trips when they learned that their Spirit Airlines flights had been canceled.

item.title: Planning a World Cup Watch Party at a Bar? The ‘FIFA Police’ Are Lurking
item.description: Some businesses advertising watch parties in Canada’s two host cities are wary of running afoul of FIFA’s trademark, which protects advertising “World Cup” events.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:description: Fans watching a FIFA World Cup match between Brazil and Cameroon at a bar in Toronto in 2014.

item.title: Most Canadians Support MAID. But the Issue of Access for People With Mental Illness Is Fraught.
item.description: As Canada weighs the future of medically assisted death, one woman fears the law will change too late for her.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Nolen
item.media:credit: Chloe Ellingson for The New York Times
item.media:description: Claire Brosseau in her Toronto home with her dog, Olive, in 2025.

item.title: After Trump’s Ousting of Maduro, Little Has Changed for Most Venezuelans
item.description: U.S. officials say they will “unleash prosperity” by commandeering the oil industry. Many people in Caracas say it will take far more than that.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak and Adriana Loureiro Fernandez
item.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
item.media:description: People ride on a crowded bus during rush hour in Caricuao, Venezuela. A one-way bus ticket costs around $0.15 — half of the nation’s hourly minimum wage.

item.title: Mexican Governor Accused by U.S. of Aiding Drug Cartel Steps Down
item.description: U.S. prosecutors accused Rubén Rocha Moya of protecting the powerful Sinaloa Cartel in exchange for bribes and political support, setting off a national scandal.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas
item.media:description: Rubén Rocha Moya, the governor of the Mexican state of Sinaloa, said he had decided to take “a temporary leave of absence.”

item.title: Why U.S. Officials Are in Venezuela Right Now
item.description: U.S. officials visited Venezuela after the first direct flight between the two countries since 2019. Our White House correspondent Tyler Pager describes how the main message of the trip was, in the words of a White House adviser “drill, baby, drill.”
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Melanie Bencosme and Todd Heisler

item.title: On May Day Cuba’s Communist Leaders Deliver a Defiant Tone to the U.S.
item.description: In front of the American Embassy in Havana, the Cuban government held its annual International Worker’s Day celebration as the United States sought to end the communist ruling system.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles
item.media:credit: Lisette Poole for The New York Times
item.media:description: May Day celebrations took place in Havana early Friday morning.

item.title: U.S. Indictment of Sinaloa’s Governor Is a Reckoning for Residents of the Mexican State
item.description: The U.S. indictment of a Mexican governor confirms what many residents say they had long suspected: The line between organized crime and the upper echelons of government has blurred.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas
item.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for The New York Times
item.media:description: Members of the Mexican National Guard in Culiacán, Sinaloa, in February.

item.title: In ‘Our Land,’ Lucrecia Martel Turns Her Camera on a Killing
item.description: Technology, history and human lives intersect in Lucrecia Martel’s examination of the death of an Indigenous leader in Argentina.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson

item.title: After Seven Years, First U.S. Direct Flight Lands in Caracas
item.description: President Trump banned commercial flights to Venezuela during his first term, but the flights are the latest step in re-establishing ties between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.media:description: The first direct flight between the United States and Venezuela landed in Caracas on Thursday afternoon.

item.title: Sheinbaum’s Dilemma in Mexico: Defy the U.S. or Arrest an Ally
item.description: The Trump administration wants President Claudia Sheinbaum to arrest a Mexican governor. She is faced with few good options in response.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas and Emiliano Rodríguez Mega
item.media:description: President Claudia Sheinbaum of Mexico could arrest one of her party allies, the governor of Sinaloa state, and extradite him to the United States, or defy the U.S. request and protect him.

item.title: U.S. Indictment Accuses Mexican Governor of Conspiring With Sinaloa Cartel
item.description: Prosecutors accused Rubén Rocha Moya, the governor of Sinaloa state, and other Mexican officials of a yearslong conspiracy to protect the powerful cartel.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas, Maria Abi-Habib and Emiliano Rodríguez Mega
item.media:description: Rubén Rocha Moya, the governor of Sinaloa state, is the highest-ranking member of Mexico’s dominant political party, Morena, to be indicted by the United States.

item.title: Epstein Obtained Objects From Islam’s Holiest Site for His Island ‘Mosque’
item.description: Jeffrey Epstein’s messages cast light on an unusual building on his private island and show how his connections helped him secure tapestries from Mecca for it.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
